item.title: Who's pushing the couriers?
item.description: A friend got hit on her bike. She's a courier. On piece rates, subcontractor shadow fleets, a BG-Verkehr accident statistic — and the antidote: bringing the pace down. With a mori set running in the background.
item.author: Stefan-Olav Hüllinghorst

item.title: Calling things what they are
item.description: A new post on the legal question of naming genocide and a site-wide gender-language audit: both from the same impulse.
item.author: Stefan-Olav Hüllinghorst

item.title: Calling a genocide a genocide
item.description: If denying past genocides is criminal, naming present genocides must be possible. A legal stocktake on Gaza, Xinjiang, Tigray — with a note on Salgado.
item.author: Stefan-Olav Hüllinghorst

item.title: When Germany's cyber-security agency recommends Signal
item.description: Germany's BSI recommends end-to-end encryption to at-risk groups — while the same government negotiates Chat Control at the EU level. On the irony of one arm of the state protecting exactly what the other arm wants to dismantle.
item.author: Stefan-Olav Hüllinghorst

item.title: Everyone has someone to feed
item.description: A call to police and activists alike — stay peaceful, stay respectful. On both sides of every line stands a person with someone waiting at home.
item.author: Stefan-Olav Hüllinghorst

item.title: Mirror live — everything at once
item.description: One commit. Six new subpages. RSS. Comments. A daily automated post agent. No phased rollout.
item.author: Stefan-Olav Hüllinghorst

item.title: One night, six pillars
item.description: In one night, the personal site grew a frame: Philosophy, Science, Ethics, Mentorship, Tribe, Movement. Plus a movement that launches on May 1. The soundtrack fits.
item.author: Stefan-Olav Hüllinghorst

item.title: The Three Guardians of 2FA
item.description: After the Authy meltdown and the ongoing LastPass fallout: 22 authenticator apps compared. Three survive, five get kicked out.
item.author: Stefan-Olav Hüllinghorst

item.title: ICE 541, from little-b to big-B
item.description: €90 ticket. €11.50 coffee and a roll. Free of charge: getting barked at in the dining car by someone in the worst possible mood. Service means SERVICE.
item.author: Stefan-Olav Hüllinghorst
